properties.remark: DRIFTING SAND CLOSED 3 ROADS IN THE PALM SPRINGS AREA INCLUDING HIGHWAY 111 AT WINDY POINT.
PILOT REPORT NEAR SAN DIEGO
*** 3 INJ *** MULTI VEHICLE ACCIDENT LIKELY RELATED TO LOW VISIBILITY IN DUST AND SUSTAINED HIGH WINDS. INJURIES ESTIMATED BASED ON MEDIA REPORT. HIGHWAY 86 AFFECTED.
LARGE BRANCHES DOWN FROM A MIMOSA TREE.
REPORT OF PHELAN ROAD CLOSED WEST AT BEEKLEY ROAD. POSSIBLE CLOSURE OF HIGHWAY 138. HEAV RAIN
POWER LINES DOWN AT ETHANAC ROAD AND EVANS ROAD WEST OF ROMOLAND AROUND 300 PM DUE TO WIND.
POWER LINES DOWN AT SOUTH D AND EAST 7TH STREETS IN PERRIS DUE TO WIND.
POWER LINES DOWN IN THE 27900 BLOCK OF WASHINGTON STREET AROUND 300 PM DUE TO WIND.
SPOTTER REPORT
SPOTTER REPORT
POWERLINES DOWN ON 7TH AND D STREETS DUE TO WIND KNOCKING TREES ON LINES
3 POWER POLES REPORTED DOWN BY WIND. ANZA BORREGO STATE PARK RANGERS.
LONE PINE CANYON ROAD NUMEROUS DEBRIS FLOWS
A THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST MOST LIKELY CAUSED MULTIPLE POWER OUTAGES FROM 1230 PM TO 200 PM. HEAVY RAINFALL AND 1/4 INCH HAIL WERE ALSO REPORTED.
STREET FLOODING 6-12 INCHES DEEP ON BIG BEAR BLVD FROM STANFIELD CUTOFF TO PINE KNOT AVE AND ALSO RATHBUN CREEK AT CATALINA RD.
CHIHUAHUA VALLEY RD IMPASSABLE DUE TO FLOODING. WATER RUNNING FAST OVER ROAD. TWO FEET DEEP IN PLACES. OTHER ROADS ARE WASHED OUT NEARBY.
CAMPO ASOS
CAMPO ASOS RECORDED A WIND GUST OF 61 MPH AT 252 PM.
SEVERAL LARGE TREE BRANCHES FELL ONTO PROPERTY.
SDGE CMCSD
TWO REPORTS FROM THE PUBLIC OF QUARTER-SIZED HAIL IN DANA POINT AROUND 700 AM.
HAIL ONE INCH IN DIAMETER, OR THE SIZE OF A QUARTER IN EAST COSTA MESA.
TWO REPORTS FROM THE PUBLIC OF QUARTER-SIZED HAIL IN ANAHEIM BETWEEN ABOUT 1000 AND 1030 AM.
4 TO 5 INCHES OF STANDING WATER ON HIGHWAY 18 BETWEEN DEAD MANS POINT AND HIGHWAY 247. MULTIPLE VEHICLES STALLED DUE TO WATER INTAKE.
HIGHWAY 18 CLOSED AT BEAR VALLEY ROAD JUST EAST OF APPLE VALLEY DUE TO 1-2 FEET OF WATER ON THE ROADWAY BETWEEN HIGH ROAD AND MILE MARKER 80. NUMEROUS VEHICLES STALLED DUE TO WATER INTAKE.
MINOR FLOODING TO SIX HOUSES AND AN ELEMENTARY SCHOOL DURING HEAVY RAIN
BOX CANYON ROAD, JUST SOUTH OF JOSHUA TREE NATIONAL MONUMENT, ROADWAY IS FLOODED
ROCKS AND MUD COVERING HIGHWAY 74 NEAR INTERSECTION WITH SANTA ROSA TRUCK TRAIL
8 INCHES OF MUD COVERING HIGHWAY 111 AT PALM DITCH BRIDGE
PINON HILLS PICTURE OF MUD AND DEBRIS ON ROAD. ROCKS AS LARGE AS ORANGES ON ROAD AND EQUIPMENT USED TO REMOVE DEBRIS. FLOODING IN ANTELOPE VALLEY HILLS NEAR HIGHWAY 138.
COUNTY ROAD CREWS HAD TO CLEAN SOME DEBRIS OFF HIGHWAY 79 ALONG THE RIVERSIDE AND SAN DIEGO COUNTY LINE
SHEEP CREEK CROSSING AT LONE PINE CANYON ROAD CLOSED DUE TO A FLOW OF MUD, ROCK AND TREE DEBRIS. SPOTTER SBD265 IN WRIGHTWOOD REPORTED A HALF-INCH OF RAIN IN 30 MINUTES.
AT 402 PM, THE CHP PAGE IS REPORTING THAT A TRUCK WITH A CAMPING TRAILER ROLLED OVER. THE CHP IS REPORTING THAT THE WINDS CAUSED THE ACCIDENT.
BMSSD
THREE WATERSPOUTS SPOTTED 1 MILE OFF WINDANDSEA BEACH MOVING FROM SOUTHWEST TO NORTHEAST FROM 1130 TO 1140 AM. NO LANDFALL NOTED.
1120 AM MULTIPLE WATER SPOUTS SPOTTED 2 MILES WEST OF LA JOLLA SHORES.
